BIMNISTRY 0T AGRICUL {(DEPARTMENT OF AGRIQULTURAL RESEARCH AND EDUCATION) (CENTRAL AGRICULTURAL UNIVERAITY, "TMPHAL) Manipur, the 22nd March 1995 ORDINANCE Y6/ (GIYRRAY PLVO/OIA ON) S661 10 T ON Whereas the sanction of the Cenirul Agricultural Universtiy Scheme under tbe VIIIih Five-Yenr Plan, as com- mun by the Joinl Seorctury, Department of Agricultui) Researeh and Education and Sccrstary, Indian Council of Agriculiural Rescarch, vide lelter nombor [(2) 92-Plan (Part- L), duled the 13th Octobor, 1993 provides for the astublish- ment of the Ceatral Agricultural University with headquaiicrs at Imphal and seven constituent Colleges spread over six states —College of Agriculture (Manipur), College of Veterinary Sciences (Mizoram), College of Horticulture and Forestry (Aranachal Pradesh), College of Kishcries (Tripom), College of Apricultoral Engineering apd Post-Flurvest Techno- logy (Sikkim), Cpllege of Home Scicnce and College of Post- Graduate Studies (Meghalaya). b ‘Wherens the aforesaid sanction also provides for creation THE GAZETTE OF INDIA, JULY 22, 1995 (ASADHA 31, 1917) * confained in Appendig-IT of this And wheiens Uhe Govemment of Tadia o the Depagtent of Agricalnal Research and Bducation/ladian Conneil of Agricultural Research has approved to make an Ordinunce regurding the appointments and emoluments of cmployecs other than thoss for whom provision has been made in the it gricaltural_Unlversity, Imphal vide Shatite; he—Central—As laiter[glo. 42-1/94 Fdn.lV, dated [4th Murch, 1993 R Naw, therefore, in exerciso of the powers conferred by Sub'Saction (2) read with clause (i) of Sub-section (1) of Seclion 27 of the Centrul Agricultural University Act, 1992 (No. 40 of 1992) and with previous approval of the Centra) Government, s aforesnid, L the Viee-Chancellor of the Central Agricoltoral Universily. Tmphal, do hereby make this fst Ordinance and notify that the various non-lenching pesitions of the Universily as contuiped In Appendix shal carry the pay seales and bo filled up following the guildelines ordinance und the proge- dures as fald down hereunder, 1. The differcnt sanctioned non-teaching position of the University with the scole of pay shall be one 88 contained in Appendix-l of this Ordinance. The Vice-Chancellor, how- over, shall buve the nuthority to transfer ote or more ~posi- tion(s) with or without the person occopying the gaid position(s), from ope wnit/constituent collogo to angther In and tho filing up of both ncademic and nomncademic/ supporting stadf as provided therein to yun the ' university the interest of the University work. and Ity constituent colleges with the necessary modiflea- } . tion(s), if any, considored necessary by the Central Agricul- 2. All posts carrying pay scale of Rs. 2200--4000/- and above, the appointing authority shall be the Vice-Chancellor with the approval of the Board of Management of the Uni- versity and for the post carrying grade lower than Rs, 2200 4000/- the Vice-Chancellor shall bo the approving and the anpointing authority. Mowmber Notwithstanding anything contained in the provisions made above, the Vico-Chincellor may nominate an ofticer of the University to preside over the meetings of the Selcetion Commi‘tee in respoct of Group ‘A’ posts. Also if the posta of Directors are vacant or for some resson he is not in a0 position to preside over the mestings of the Seloction Com- mittes in respect of Group ‘B’ positions the Vice-Chancellor whall normally presido over tho meeting(s). For similar reason the mectlngs of the Selection Committee In respect of the Group ‘C’ positiona shall be presided by one of the Doins to be pominated by the Vice-Chancellor. 6, The undermentioned procedures shall be followed by the Solectlon Committee(s) while ovuluating the candidates called for interview and for making recommendation(s) : (1) Each Sclection Committee shall decide its own method(s) and procedure(s) for ugsessment of the suitability and making recommendation of the candi- dafes on the busls of the guidelines, if any, issued by the University and tho qualifications and ofber ollgibllity criterla aa contalned in Appendix-Tl of this Ordinance, (ii) OF the total posts to bo filled by direct recrultment in a purticular cadre (Postwise) in u singlo calendar vear 22.5 pér cerit shall be reserved for tho scheduled casle and scheduled tribe candidates posscsalng the 27 per cent shall be resorved for other Backward Clas- prescribed qualification(s) and exporience and ses (OBCs) as per the Govt. of Indiu guidelines, (ifi) Ta case of promotion, whers test or persoml inter- view is not considered necessary, panel may be drawn up by the Selectlon Commliteo on the basis of usscss- ment of the records of works and of conduc! of the employee(s) concerned. 1f the service of employees working in the service of Contral/Stats Govornment Public Seclor Under!akings, statutory bodles Including ICAR wad (iv) or -- 3 of 20 -- Parr I1I--Sgc. 4] THE GAZETTE OF INDIA, JULY 22, 1995 _(ASADHA 31, 1917) 1215 agriculturel universlties, ele. aro taken on deputu- tion, their services ahall be governed by the standard terms and conditions of deputation as applicable in the Gowt. of India. 7. Procedure for Temporary Appointment : Temporary appolaiment(s) and appolntment(s) for a fixed pertod shall be made by the concemed authority 5o the man- ner prescribed below : « (a) If the temporary vacancy is for a duration longer than one year, it shall be filled on tho recommenda- tlon of the Selection Commitles in accordance with the procedure indicated in the forgoing clauses of this Ordinance. Provided that if the Vice-Chancellor is satisfied that dn the intercats of work it is necsssery to fill a vacancy immedintoly, the uppointment(s) may be made on adhoc busls for a period not exceeding five montbs in zespect of Group 'C' and ‘D' posts only on the recommendation of a local Selection Commiittee consisting of Director/Dean, Registrar or his nominec and one nomince of (he Vice- Chancglior. No extension in service beyond five months shall be granted to the employes(s) appoint- ¢d by thls procedure. (b) No employee appointed purely on temporary basis shall be continued In service beyond five months period unless he is recommonded for appointment on the said post or any position by the appropriale Sclection Commitiee, 8. Bmoluments of the employces and terms and couditions of service : Employees of the Contral Agricultural Uhiversity will be entitlod to the puy scales and other allowances preacribed by the University from time to time and they will be governed by the Service Rules of the University. 9. Age of Retirement Every non-teaching and supporting employee of tho Uni- versity shell retire on tho lut duy of tho month in which he/aho shall bo attalning the age of 58 (fifty-cight) yoars. 10. Probation All newly appointed /promoted pereonnel Initially shall be on probation for a perlod of two yoars. If fho porfor mance of any probationer le not found satistactory tho appoint. ing au'horily by specific order may exten. the perlod of probi- tion or his/her sorvice may be dispensed with or he/sho may be roverted to his/hor provious position. 11. Age limits for Direct Recruitment The minimum age limit for appolntiknt to any position in the University shall be 18 (cighteen) years und the muxi- mum age limit 35 (thirty five) yoars. The maximum age Jimlt shall, however, be relaxable for SC/ST candidates ns per the Central Government rules and for those altendy in Government /Semi-Govermment/ICAR /University Service. 12. Every duly appointed rogular employee of the Manipur Agrlcultural College, mphal who has been in employment In the Colloge boforo the date of take over of the College by the Contral Agriculural Universlty (22-11-93) and has be- come the employes of the Universlty from the stid date shall hold office with sexvice protection under the University with such rights and privileges as contributry provident fund, pratulty, leave, medical bonefils otc. ay admissible to him/ ber immediately beforo the take over of the said college by the Central Agricullural University. The employeo shall also conlinue to be in the sorvice of lhe Central Agricultural Uni- versity with their old designations with grades/pay scales ¢njoyed by them immediately before lhe take over of the colfege. 1f their grades of pay immediately before tho take over of the college are different from tho one(s) provided under this Ordinance, ‘hey shall contoinue to work in thoir reapeotive grades na “Porsonal Grades” till they, on the busis of direct recruitment/promotion, happen to occupy u different position. 1In the lafter case, the designation privilegea and terms and conditions of service shall bo same as presctibed for the employces of the Central Agricultural University. 13. In all matters, not provided in the ordinunce, till the framing of the detalled sorvice conditions for the employee of the Central Agricultural University, the conditions of the service of the employees of the University shall temperorily bo governed by the rules applicable fo the employees of the ICAR working in the North-Eastern Reglon. After enforce- ment of the new service rules of tho Central Agricultural University, the servico conditions of the employees of tho Central Agricultural Univeraity shall be governed by the new rules automatically. 14, The provision(s) of this Ordinance shall como into effect from the date of its publication in the official Guzelto.
